mission.title: cri-o: Support pulling an image specified by both tag and digest
mission.description: Support pulling an image specified by both tag and digest. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current cri-o deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cri-o version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cri-o -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cri-o
helm list -n cri-o 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cri-o installation.

steps.title: Review cri-o configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cri-o configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cri-o -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cri-o
kubectl get configmap -n cri-o -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cri-o
```
**Description**

Hi all! Currently trying to get skaffold to work. Skaffold works by pulling an image specified by both tag and digest.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support pulling an image specified by both tag and digest
steps.description:
Favour the digest over the tag if both specified.
This is required for docker compatibility.

_Docker supports canonical image references (`repo/image:tag`) that contain both a tag and an image ID. The latter is actually used for resolution. Despite its ambiguity this feature is used by tools like
```yaml
root@a1:~# crio --version
crio version 1.13.1-dev
commit: ""
```

steps.title: Upgrade cri-o to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade cri-o:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade cri-o cri-o/cri-o --namespace cri-o
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cri-o
helm list -n cri-o
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cri-o -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cri-o
kubectl get events -n cri-o --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support pulling an image specified by both tag and digest" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Favour the digest over the tag if both specified.
This is required for docker compatibility.

_Docker supports canonical image references (`repo/image:tag`) that contain both a tag and an image ID. The latter is actually used for resolution.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cri-o installed or the issue environment reproducible.
